title: Increasing Public Access to Recreation Act
short_title: Increasing Public Access to Recreation Act
plain_summary: This bill makes more money available for creating recreational activities on land owned by the federal government. The money comes from something called the Land and Water Conservation Fund. The bill increases the percentage of money that can be used for these projects from three percent to ten percent. It also raises the total dollar amount that can be spent from fifteen million dollars to fifty million dollars each year. This means more parks, trails, and outdoor recreation areas can be built on federal land for people to enjoy.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Increasing Public Access to Recreation Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Increase for Recreational Public Access
sections.text: This section changes an existing law about recreation on federal land. It makes two changes to section two hundred three thousand and six of title fifty four of the United States Code. First, it changes three percent to ten percent. Second, it changes fifteen million dollars to fifty million dollars.
